calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-01-2012 Vacated

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-03-2012 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-04-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS UNIVERSAL PARAGON CORPORATION AND SUNQUEST PROPERTIES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Off calendar per the notice filed by the moving parties on March 15, 2012. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Marc A. Zeppetello, Esq., of Barg Coffin Lewis & Trapp LLP, Tel. 415.228.5496, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party.
Stuart I. Block, Esq., of Cox Castle & Nicholson LLP, Tel. 415.392.4200, appearing for Defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. The court vacated its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF UNION PACIFIC RAILROAD COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT was treated as a motion for summary adjudication. Motion for summary adjudication is granted in favor of plantiff and against defendants. Mr. Zeppetello to prepare and submit a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-29-2011 Set For Trial On Oct-15-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-28-2011 Continued To Jan-13-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARC A. ZEPPETELLO OF BARG, COFFIN LEWIS & TRAPP, TEL. NO. 228-5400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STUART I. BLOCK OF COX CASTLE NICHOLSON LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 262-5105 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS UNIVERSAL PARAGON CORPORATION AND SUNQUEST PROPERTIES INCORPORATED.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendants Universal Paragpn Corporation and Sunquest Properties, Incorporated's demurrer to amended complaint is overruled with ten days leave to answer. The statute of limitations period on plaintiff's claims does not commence until defendants have refused to perform their obligations under the indemnity agreements. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)
